verses.latin_vulgate: Addens quoque Eliu, hæc locutus est :
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיֹּ֥סֶף אֱלִיה֗וּא וַיֹּאמַֽר׃
verses.king_james: Elihu also proceeded, and said,

verses.latin_vulgate: Sustine me paululum, et indicabo tibi :  adhuc enim habeo quod pro Deo loquar.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: כַּתַּר־לִ֣י זְ֭עֵיר וַאֲחַוֶּ֑ךָּ כִּ֤י ע֖וֹד לֶאֱל֣וֹהַּ מִלִּֽים׃
verses.king_james: Suffer me a little, and I will shew thee that I have yet to speak on God’s behalf.

verses.latin_vulgate: Repetam scientiam meam a principio,  et operatorem meum probabo justum.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: אֶשָּׂ֣א דֵ֭עִי לְמֵרָח֑וֹק וּ֝לְפֹעֲלִ֗י אֶֽתֵּֽן־צֶֽדֶק׃
verses.king_james: I will fetch my knowledge from afar, and will ascribe righteousness to my Maker.

verses.latin_vulgate: Vere enim absque mendacio sermones mei,  et perfecta scientia probabitur tibi.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: כִּֽי־אׇ֭מְנָם לֹא־שֶׁ֣קֶר מִלָּ֑י תְּמִ֖ים דֵּע֣וֹת עִמָּֽךְ׃
verses.king_james: For truly my words shall not be false: he that is perfect in knowledge is with thee.

verses.latin_vulgate: Deus potentes non abjicit,  cum et ipse sit potens :
verses.hebrew_masoretic: הֶן־אֵ֣ל כַּ֭בִּיר וְלֹ֣א יִמְאָ֑ס כַּ֝בִּ֗יר כֹּ֣חַֽ לֵֽב׃
verses.king_james: Behold, God is mighty, and despiseth not any: he is mighty in strength and wisdom.

verses.latin_vulgate: sed non salvat impios,  et judicium pauperibus tribuit.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: לֹא־יְחַיֶּ֥ה רָשָׁ֑ע וּמִשְׁפַּ֖ט עֲנִיִּ֣ים יִתֵּֽן׃
verses.king_james: He preserveth not the life of the wicked: but giveth right to the poor.

verses.latin_vulgate: Non auferet a justo oculos suos :  et reges in solio collocat in perpetuum,  et illi eriguntur.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: לֹֽא־יִגְרַ֥ע מִצַּדִּ֗יק עֵ֫ינָ֥יו וְאֶת־מְלָכִ֥ים לַכִּסֵּ֑א וַיֹּשִׁיבֵ֥ם לָ֝נֶ֗צַח וַיִּגְבָּֽהוּ׃
verses.king_james: He withdraweth not his eyes from the righteous: but with kings are they on the throne; yea, he doth establish them for ever, and they are exalted.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et si fuerint in catenis,  et vinciantur funibus paupertatis,
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְאִם־אֲסוּרִ֥ים בַּזִּקִּ֑ים יִ֝לָּכְד֗וּן בְּחַבְלֵי־עֹֽנִי׃
verses.king_james: And if they be bound in fetters, and be holden in cords of affliction;

verses.latin_vulgate: indicabit eis opera eorum,  et scelera eorum, quia violenti fuerunt.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיַּגֵּ֣ד לָהֶ֣ם פׇּעֳלָ֑ם וּ֝פִשְׁעֵיהֶ֗ם כִּ֣י יִתְגַּבָּֽרוּ׃
verses.king_james: Then he sheweth them their work, and their transgressions that they have exceeded.

verses.latin_vulgate: Revelabit quoque aurem eorum, ut corripiat :  et loquetur, ut revertantur ab iniquitate.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיִּ֣גֶל אׇ֭זְנָם לַמּוּסָ֑ר וַ֝יֹּ֗אמֶר כִּֽי־יְשׁוּב֥וּן מֵאָֽוֶן׃
verses.king_james: He openeth also their ear to discipline, and commandeth that they return from iniquity.

verses.latin_vulgate: Si audierint et observaverint, complebunt dies suos in bono,  et annos suos in gloria :
verses.hebrew_masoretic: אִֽם־יִשְׁמְע֗וּ וְֽיַ֫עֲבֹ֥דוּ יְכַלּ֣וּ יְמֵיהֶ֣ם בַּטּ֑וֹב וּ֝שְׁנֵיהֶ֗ם בַּנְּעִימִֽים׃
verses.king_james: If they obey and serve him, they shall spend their days in prosperity, and their years in pleasures.

verses.latin_vulgate: si autem non audierint,  transibunt per gladium,  et consumentur in stultitia.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְאִם־לֹ֣א יִ֭שְׁמְעוּ בְּשֶׁ֣לַח יַעֲבֹ֑רוּ וְ֝יִגְוְע֗וּ בִּבְלִי־דָֽעַת׃
verses.king_james: But if they obey not, they shall perish by the sword, and they shall die without knowledge.

verses.latin_vulgate: Simulatores et callidi provocant iram Dei,  neque clamabunt cum vincti fuerint.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְֽחַנְפֵי־לֵ֭ב יָשִׂ֣ימוּ אָ֑ף לֹ֥א יְ֝שַׁוְּע֗וּ כִּ֣י אֲסָרָֽם׃
verses.king_james: But the hypocrites in heart heap up wrath: they cry not when he bindeth them.

verses.latin_vulgate: Morietur in tempestate anima eorum,  et vita eorum inter effeminatos.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: תָּמֹ֣ת בַּנֹּ֣עַר נַפְשָׁ֑ם וְ֝חַיָּתָ֗ם בַּקְּדֵשִֽׁים׃
verses.king_james: They die in youth, and their life is among the unclean.

verses.latin_vulgate: Eripiet de angustia sua pauperem,  et revelabit in tribulatione aurem ejus.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: יְחַלֵּ֣ץ עָנִ֣י בְעׇנְי֑וֹ וְיִ֖גֶל בַּלַּ֣חַץ אׇזְנָֽם׃
verses.king_james: He delivereth the poor in his affliction, and openeth their ears in oppression.

verses.latin_vulgate: Igitur salvabit te de ore angusto latissime,  et non habente fundamentum subter se :  requies autem mensæ tuæ erit plena pinguedine.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְאַ֤ף הֲסִֽיתְךָ֨ ׀ מִפִּי־צָ֗ר רַ֭חַב לֹא־מוּצָ֣ק תַּחְתֶּ֑יהָ וְנַ֥חַת שֻׁ֝לְחָנְךָ֗ מָ֣לֵא דָֽשֶׁן׃
verses.king_james: Even so would he have removed thee out of the strait into a broad place, where there is no straitness; and that which should be set on thy table should be full of fatness.

verses.latin_vulgate: Causa tua quasi impii judicata est :  causam judiciumque recipies.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְדִין־רָשָׁ֥ע מָלֵ֑אתָ דִּ֖ין וּמִשְׁפָּ֣ט יִתְמֹֽכוּ׃
verses.king_james: But thou hast fulfilled the judgment of the wicked: judgment and justice take hold on thee.

verses.latin_vulgate: Non te ergo superet ira ut aliquem opprimas :  nec multitudo donorum inclinet te.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: כִּֽי־חֵ֭מָה פֶּן־יְסִֽיתְךָ֣ בְסָ֑פֶק וְרׇב־כֹּ֝֗פֶר אַל־יַטֶּֽךָּ׃
verses.king_james: Because there is wrath, beware lest he take thee away with his stroke: then a great ransom cannot deliver thee.

verses.latin_vulgate: Depone magnitudinem tuam absque tribulatione,  et omnes robustos fortitudine.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: הֲיַעֲרֹ֣ךְ שׁ֭וּעֲךָ לֹ֣א בְצָ֑ר וְ֝כֹ֗ל מַאֲמַצֵּי־כֹֽחַ׃
verses.king_james: Will he esteem thy riches? no, not gold, nor all the forces of strength.

verses.latin_vulgate: Ne protrahas noctem,  ut ascendant populi pro eis.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: אַל־תִּשְׁאַ֥ף הַלָּ֑יְלָה לַעֲל֖וֹת עַמִּ֣ים תַּחְתָּֽם׃
verses.king_james: Desire not the night, when people are cut off in their place.

verses.latin_vulgate: Cave ne declines ad iniquitatem :  hanc enim cœpisti sequi post miseriam.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: הִ֭שָּׁמֶר אַל־תֵּ֣פֶן אֶל־אָ֑וֶן כִּֽי־עַל־זֶ֝֗ה בָּחַ֥רְתָּ מֵעֹֽנִי׃
verses.king_james: Take heed, regard not iniquity: for this hast thou chosen rather than affliction.

verses.latin_vulgate: Ecce Deus excelsus in fortitudine sua,  et nullus ei similis in legislatoribus.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: הֶן־אֵ֭ל יַשְׂגִּ֣יב בְּכֹח֑וֹ מִ֖י כָמֹ֣הוּ מוֹרֶֽה׃
verses.king_james: Behold, God exalteth by his power: who teacheth like him?

verses.latin_vulgate: Quis poterit scrutari vias ejus ?  aut quis potest ei dicere : Operatus es iniquitatem ?
verses.hebrew_masoretic: מִֽי־פָקַ֣ד עָלָ֣יו דַּרְכּ֑וֹ וּמִי־אָ֝מַ֗ר פָּעַ֥לְתָּ עַוְלָֽה׃
verses.king_james: Who hath enjoined him his way? or who can say, Thou hast wrought iniquity?

verses.latin_vulgate: Memento quod ignores opus ejus,  de quo cecinerunt viri.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: זְ֭כֹר כִּֽי־תַשְׂגִּ֣יא פׇעֳל֑וֹ אֲשֶׁ֖ר שֹׁרְר֣וּ אֲנָשִֽׁים׃
verses.king_james: Remember that thou magnify his work, which men behold.

verses.latin_vulgate: Omnes homines vident eum :  unusquisque intuetur procul.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: כׇּל־אָדָ֥ם חָזוּ־ב֑וֹ אֱ֝נ֗וֹשׁ יַבִּ֥יט מֵרָחֽוֹק׃
verses.king_james: Every man may see it; man may behold it afar off.

verses.latin_vulgate: Ecce Deus magnus vincens scientiam nostram :  numerus annorum ejus inæstimabilis.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: הֶן־אֵ֣ל שַׂ֭גִּיא וְלֹ֣א נֵדָ֑ע מִסְפַּ֖ר שָׁנָ֣יו וְלֹא־חֵֽקֶר׃
verses.king_james: Behold, God is great, and we know him not, neither can the number of his years be searched out.

verses.latin_vulgate: Qui aufert stillas pluviæ,  et effundit imbres ad instar gurgitum,
verses.hebrew_masoretic: כִּ֭י יְגָרַ֣ע נִטְפֵי־מָ֑יִם יָזֹ֖קּוּ מָטָ֣ר לְאֵדֽוֹ׃
verses.king_james: For he maketh small the drops of water: they pour down rain according to the vapour thereof:

verses.latin_vulgate: qui de nubibus fluunt  quæ prætexunt cuncta desuper.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: אֲשֶֽׁר־יִזְּל֥וּ שְׁחָקִ֑ים יִ֝רְעֲפ֗וּ עֲלֵ֤י ׀ אָדָ֬ם רָֽב׃
verses.king_james: Which the clouds do drop and distil upon man abundantly.

verses.latin_vulgate: Si voluerit extendere nubes quasi tentorium suum,
verses.hebrew_masoretic: אַ֣ף אִם־יָ֭בִין מִפְרְשֵׂי־עָ֑ב תְּ֝שֻׁא֗וֹת סֻכָּתֽוֹ׃
verses.king_james: Also can any understand the spreadings of the clouds, or the noise of his tabernacle?

verses.latin_vulgate: et fulgurare lumine suo desuper,  cardines quoque maris operiet.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: הֵן־פָּרַ֣שׂ עָלָ֣יו אוֹר֑וֹ וְשׇׁרְשֵׁ֖י הַיָּ֣ם כִּסָּֽה׃
verses.king_james: Behold, he spreadeth his light upon it, and covereth the bottom of the sea.

verses.latin_vulgate: Per hæc enim judicat populos,  et dat escas multis mortalibus.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: כִּֽי־בָ֭ם יָדִ֣ין עַמִּ֑ים יִֽתֶּן־אֹ֥כֶל לְמַכְבִּֽיר׃
verses.king_james: For by them judgeth he the people; he giveth meat in abundance.

verses.latin_vulgate: In manibus abscondit lucem,  et præcepit ei ut rursus adveniat.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: עַל־כַּפַּ֥יִם כִּסָּה־א֑וֹר וַיְצַ֖ו עָלֶ֣יהָ בְמַפְגִּֽיעַ׃
verses.king_james: With clouds he covereth the light; and commandeth it not to shine by the cloud that cometh betwixt.

verses.latin_vulgate: Annuntiat de ea amico suo, quod possessio ejus sit,  et ad eam possit ascendere.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: יַגִּ֣יד עָלָ֣יו רֵע֑וֹ מִ֝קְנֶ֗ה אַ֣ף עַל־עוֹלֶֽה׃
verses.king_james: The noise thereof sheweth concerning it, the cattle also concerning the vapour.
